user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate the TC387 GTM TOM 3‑phase inverter PWM to TC4D7 (KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE) using eGTM Cluster 1, ATOM0 CH0/CH1/CH2, with 20 kHz center‑aligned complementary PWM and 1 µs deadtime via DTM/CDTM, synchronized start and synchronized duty updates, and a single period IRQ on CPU0 priority 20 toggling LED P13.0; enable eGTM GCLK and FXCLK0; provide independent U/V/W duty updates via float32[3] and basic error/status flags using IfxEgtm_Pwm.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate three complementary, center‑aligned PWM pairs (U/V/W) on eGTM C1 ATOM0 CH0/1/2 with 1 µs hardware deadtime using CDTM, start all channels synchronously, apply duty updates synchronously at period boundary, and trigger a single period interrupt on CPU0 (priority 20) that toggles LED P13.0. Provide a function to update duty array float32 duty[3] and a clear error/status reporting mechanism: public APIs return Ifx_Status (Ok/NotOk), and the module exposes an EgtmAtom3phInv_Status struct (via getEgtmAtom3phInvStatus()) with flags {initDone, pinmapOk, irqEnabled} and a lastError enum {EGTMPWM_OK, EGTMPWM_ERR_PINMAP, EGTMPWM_ERR_PARAM, EGTMPWM_ERR_HW}.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified TC4xx eGTM PWM driver supports multi‑channel synchronized PWM, complementary outputs, hardware deadtime via DTM, and center‑aligned mode. It matches the advanced motor inverter requirement and is available in the template.

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Apply synchronized duty cycle updates for U/V/W (float32 duty[3]) at the PWM period boundary across all three ATOM0 channels.

test_interfaces.mock_strategy: Weak-linked wrapper functions replace direct hardware calls for unit tests; wrappers can be overridden by mocks.

driver_changes: IfxGtm_Pwm -> IfxEgtm_Pwm (unified high-level PWM on TC4xx)

api_changes.reason: TC3xx→TC4xx naming and unified eGTM PWM API
Watchdog service migration to IfxWtu on TC4xx
Use synchronized period update on TC4xx unified driver
ISR naming aligned to eGTM/ATOM on TC4xx

migration_notes.context_preserved: The solution keeps eGTM Cluster 1 ATOM0 CH0/1/2 for the 3-phase inverter, configures center-aligned complementary PWM with 1 µs deadtime via unified IfxEgtm_Pwm, enables eGTM GCLK/FXCLK0 using IfxEgtm_Cmu, performs synchronized start and duty updates, and installs a single period ISR on CPU0 priority 20 toggling LED P13.0. Pin mapping uses IfxEgtm_PinMap (timer pinmap migration), and the software interface provides float32 duty[3] updates and status/error flags. If original P20.x pins are not available on the target package, error_pinmap can be set and the provided verified P02.x TOUT mapping is used. Pin mapping runtime handling is defined: initEgtmAtom3phInv returns NotOk and sets status.lastError=EGTMPWM_ERR_PINMAP if requested TOUT pins are unavailable; if error_pinmap=true, a verified fallback P02.x TOUT mapping is attempted and status.pinmapOk is updated; on repeated failure an assert or user-provided error hook is invoked based on build configuration.
